steps.title: Use the cost contrast
steps.content: Radar and sonar are described as too costly for consumer use. The Wi-Fi alternative is then described as giving users a cheap way to detect people or gestures, so the blank must express low cost.

steps.title: Reject unrelated qualities
steps.content: Choice A concerns whether something is obvious, not whether it is affordable. Choice C means rare, which is unrelated to the cost contrast. Choice D means not practical, but the passage presents the Wi-Fi approach as useful. The needed word means not expensive. The answer is B.
